content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/381560448_Analysis_of_the_influence_of_damp-heat_aging_on_the_shear_failure_of_the_adhesive_joint_of_composite_materials", "title": "Analysis of the influence of damp-heat aging on the shear failure of ...", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12348657/", "title": "Comprehensive Evaluation and Analysis of Aging Performance of ...", "summary": "Abstract In civil engineering, with the increasing demand for structural reinforcement and renovation projects, polymer-rich anchoring adhesives have attracted much attention due to their performance advantage of having high strength and have become a key factor in ensuring the safety and durability of buildings. In this study, polymer-rich anchoring adhesives underwent three artificial aging treatments (alkali medium, hygrothermal, and water bath) to evaluate their aging performance. Alkali treatment reduced bending strength by up to 70% (sample 5#) within 500 h before stabilizing, while hygrothermal and water-curing treatments caused reductions of 16\u201351% and 15\u201377%, respectively, depending on adhesive composition.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Alkali treatment reduced bending strength by up to 70% (sample 5#) within 500 h before stabilizing, while hygrothermal and water-curing treatments caused reductions of 16\u201351% and 15\u201377%, respectively, depending on adhesive composition.", "Dynamic thermomechanical analysis revealed significant loss factor decreases (e.g., epoxy adhesives dropped from >1.0 to stable lower values after 500 h aging), indicating increased rigidity.", "Infrared spectroscopy confirmed chemical degradation, including ester group breakage in vinyl ester resins (peak shifts at 1700 cm\u22121 and 1100 cm\u22121) and molecular chain scission in unsaturated polyesters.", "The second method involved conducting a test after implanting screws or rebar into concrete and conducting a test after slicing and artificially aging the rebar-embedded specimens [18,19,20].", "The damp and hot environment replicated the conditions that buildings experienced under natural high-temperature and high-humidity settings, such as those found in tropical rainforests or coastal areas [25,26]."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Alkali treatment reduced bending strength by up to 70% (sample 5#) within 500 h before stabilizing, while hygrothermal a"}, {"url": "https://www.ise.fraunhofer.de/content/dam/ise/de/documents/publications/conference-paper/38th-eupvsec-2021/Oez_4AV15.pdf", "title": "[PDF] The Impact of the Lamination Process on the Adhesion Properties at ...", "summary": "The investigations were conducted on small-scale laminates which were laminated with different material combinations at peak lamination temperatures from 140\u00b0C to 180\u00b0C. As a measure of the long-term durability, the laminates were exposed to damp heat (DH) up to 3000 hours. We investigated the development of the adhesion between encapsulant and glass by peel tests, the measurement of the gel content and the colour change of the backsheet by measurements of the yellowness index.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["As a measure of the long-term durability, the laminates were exposed to damp heat (DH) up to 3000 hours.", "The peel test results show that the relative maintained peel force (RMPF) varies in the range from 15.7-73.0% depending on the material and the lamination process.", "However, these modules also need to operate for long lifetimes of more than 25 or even 30 years [1,2].", "The material combinations represent common, commercially available products from the PV market. ## 2.2 Damp Heat Test The influences of the lamination process on the adhesion properties under water vapour ingress were investigated by exposing the mod", "The modules were characterized before and after the DH-test regarding their peel force, gel content and yellowness index. ## 2.3 Peel Tests The peel force at the glass-encapsulant interface is measured with the PSE peel-off instrument at an angle of "], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: As a measure of the long-term durability, the laminates were exposed to damp heat (DH) up to 3000 hours."}, {"url": "https://www.energy.gov/eere/solar/articles/degradation-study-peel-strength-mini-modules-under-damp-heat-condition", "title": "[PDF] Degradation Study of the Peel Strength of Mini-Modules Under ...", "summary": "A concept of environmental dose is established to quantify the cumulative stress suffered by PV module . A degradation model for the ## 1 Kinetic Stress Model An empirical kinetic model is developed by assuming that the rate of adhesion degradation is proportional to moisture concentration at the interface of backsheet / encapsulant and the reaction rate constant is Arrhenius dependent . It can be expressed as following : > \u2206\u0bcc > \u2206\u0be7 = \ud835\udc45 \u0bbd \u221d \ud835\udc53 \ud835\udc45\ud835\udc3b \ud835\udc52 \u0b3f > \u0cb6\u0ccc > \u0cc3\u0cc5 Where f (RH ) is a function of relative humidity ; Ea is activation energy, R is gas constant (8.314 J/ K\u00b7mol ) and T is absolute temperature in kelvin .", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["It can be expressed as following : > \u2206\u0bcc > \u2206\u0be7 = \ud835\udc45 \u0bbd \u221d \ud835\udc53 \ud835\udc45\ud835\udc3b \ud835\udc52 \u0b3f > \u0cb6\u0ccc > \u0cc3\u0cc5 Where f (RH ) is a function of relative humidity ; Ea is activation energy, R is gas constant (8.314 J/ K\u00b7mol ) and T is absolute temperature in kelvin .", "Acceleration factors around 3-6 in testing time is achieved for the other three conditions compared with that at 65 oC T and 85 % RH .", "The peel angle is 90 o and the peel speed is 50 mm/min .", "Fig . 1: Mini PV module after laser cutting . 100 mm x 120 mm, frameless P-Si Fig . 2: Peel test Arrhenius behaviour and Ea A linear relationship can be obtained by taking natural logarithm of RD and \u0b35 \u0bcd . ln \ud835\udc45 \u0bbd = \u2212 \ud835\udc38 \u0bd4 \ud835\udc45 1 \ud835\udc47 + ln (\ud835\udc58 \u2219 \ud835\udc45\ud835\udc3b ) Ln RD vs"], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: It can be expressed as following : > \u2206\u0bcc > \u2206\u0be7 = \ud835\udc45 \u0bbd \u221d \ud835\udc53 \ud835\udc45\ud835\udc3b \ud835\udc52 \u0b3f > \u0cb6\u0ccc > \u0cc3\u0cc5 Where f (RH ) is a function of relative humidity"}, {"url": "https://www.mdpi.com/2073-4360/14/9/1721", "title": "Influence of Hydrothermal Aging under Two Typical Adhesives on ...", "summary": "Introduction In recent years, due to the increasingly serious shortage of resources, automobile lightweight has become the mainstream of the world\u2019s automobile development. The lightweight body is mainly optimized from the three aspects of body structure, materials and processes to improve the strength and reduce the curb weight of the vehicle [ 1, 2]. Basalt fiber composite material has the advantages of high strength, low density, good corrosion resistance, and low cost [ 3, 4, 5].", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["The results showed that, in water or alkaline solutions, the tensile strength and interlaminar shear strength of the heat-aged BFRP board sample decreased significantly, while the tensile modulus decreased only slightly.", "Zhang et al. [ 21] studied the shear strength of adhesive joints between thin aluminum and steel substrates at different temperatures and with or without humidity.", "The following conclusions are drawn that for indoor humidity and various temperatures, there was almost no change in shear strength after 60 days of exposure, but when high temperature and high humidity (90% relative humidity) were combined, the shea", "It was observed that the tensile strength decreased by about 31.3% after moisture absorption, and decreased by 27.7% and 54.4% under high temperature (95 \u00b0C)/dry environment and high temperature (95 \u00b0C)/humid environment, respectively.", "For example, moisture absorption or cyclic absorption and desorption under high temperature and high humidity will cause voids and/or microcracks in the epoxy resin."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: The results showed that, in water or alkaline solutions, the tensile strength and interlaminar shear strength of the hea"}, {"url": "https://repository.lboro.ac.uk/articles/journal_contribution/Degradation_of_interfacial_adhesion_strength_within_photovoltaic_mini-modules_during_damp-heat_exposure/9561614/files/17193764.pdf", "title": "[PDF] Degradation of interfacial adhesion strength within photovoltaic ...", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}]}
